title: Privacy-preserving ML (federated, DP, on-device) + DSA review
focus: When each is the right answer; cost in accuracy.

items.label: Latency vs accuracy: caching, distillation, cascades
interviewQuestions: What levers do you pull when accuracy is great but latency misses the budget?
Walk through where you'd add caching in a RAG + LLM pipeline to halve P99.

items.label: Multi-model routing & cascades
interviewQuestions: How would you design a cascade: cheap model first, expensive only when needed?
What's the right verifier for the cheap model's output — and when does it dominate cost?

items.label: Cold-start strategies (recsys, search, ads)
interviewQuestions: Walk through cold-start strategies for new users vs new items.
Compare bandit-based exploration vs content-based bridges for cold start — when does each fit?

items.label: Privacy-preserving ML: federated, DP, on-device
interviewQuestions: When would you reach for federated learning vs differential privacy vs on-device inference?
What's the accuracy cost of DP-SGD at typical ε values, and how do you decide if it's acceptable?

items.label: PII handling, anonymization, k-anonymity
interviewQuestions: How would you remove PII from training data without destroying signal?
Compare hashing vs tokenization vs full redaction for PII fields — when does each fit?

items.label: Differential privacy basics
interviewQuestions: Explain differential privacy in one minute — what does ε mean?
How does DP-SGD differ from regular SGD, and what does it cost in accuracy?

items.label: Federated learning
interviewQuestions: When is federated learning the right choice over centralized training?
What are the systems-side challenges in federated learning beyond the algorithm?

items.label: Adversarial & model-extraction attacks
interviewQuestions: What is a model-extraction attack, and how do you mitigate it?
How would you defend a public ML API from membership-inference attacks?

items.label: Clarify requirements, users, constraints, and business metric for: Privacy-preserving ML (federated, DP, on-device)
interviewQuestions: What are the first five clarifying questions before proposing any model?
What is the business metric, what is the ML proxy metric, and where can they diverge?
What scale, latency, freshness, privacy, and reliability constraints change the architecture?

items.label: Defend data, labels, baseline, model choice, feature freshness, and serving path for: Privacy-preserving ML (federated, DP, on-device)
interviewQuestions: What baseline would you launch before a complex model, and how would you know it is insufficient?
Where do labels come from, what bias do they contain, and how delayed are they?
Which features must be computed online vs offline, and what happens when the feature store is slow?

items.label: Cover monitoring, rollback, cost, security, failure modes, and launch plan for: Privacy-preserving ML (federated, DP, on-device)
interviewQuestions: What online metrics, guardrails, and cohort slices decide whether this system is safe to ramp?
What is the rollback trigger, fallback experience, and incident owner if quality or latency regresses?
What are the top security, privacy, abuse, or cost risks, and what controls would you ship in v1?

references.label: Chip Huyen — Machine Learning Systems Design
Google Cloud Professional ML Engineer exam guide
Google ML system design mock interview — YouTube recommendations
